title: T-010 — API: Item detail and update (GET/PUT /api/technical/items/:item_code)
prompt:
# T-010 — API: Item detail and update (GET/PUT /api/technical/items/:item_code)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §6.2, §6.4, §6.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Item detail GET and PUT endpoints with audit and validations.

Audit log row inserts before/after JSON of changed fields and userId. Use Drizzle transactions. 404 when item_code not found in current org.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/items/service.ts [create]
- tests/api/items-detail.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Status transitions (T-011)
- BOM-side data

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given item exists in org A, when GET as org A, then 200 with related counts; when GET as org B, then 404 (RLS).
2. Given PUT updates name, when committed, then audit_log row contains old+new name.
3. Given PUT to deprecated item where field protected, when run, then 409.
4. Given invalid weight_mode update breaking V-TEC-02, when PUT runs, then 422.

## Test strategy
- RED tests: cross-org GET, audit emission on PUT, V-TEC validation on PUT.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/items-detail.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not allow PUT to change item_code (immutable after create).
- Do not return private_jsonb unless requester role is owner.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
